name: Kevon Watson
text:
Bobby Dodd Stadium isn’t the biggest in the South (places like Bryant–Denny dwarf it), but it’s got a rare, old-soul charm.
It’s literally woven into Atlanta’s skyline — you can see downtown skyscrapers rising just beyond the stands. At night, that contrast between bright field lights and the city’s glittering towers is downright cinematic.

My only complaint is the crowd, other than the student section which was very loud and active. The rest of the stadium was not. Some folks in our section behaved as if they were watching a game of golf,  even commenting about too many people standing.  Your team is undefeated! At least get on your feet for 3rd down!!

name: Subhajit Bag
text: Bobby Dodd Stadium is a great place to watch a game and experience the Georgia Tech spirit. The location in the heart of Atlanta makes the view of the skyline incredible, especially for night games. The atmosphere is energetic, with passionate fans and the band adding to the excitement. The seating is generally good, though some sections could use a bit more comfort and shade during hot days. Concessions are fairly standard but can be pricey. Overall, it’s a historic and vibrant venue that makes for a memorable game day experience. Four stars because while it’s fantastic, there’s still room for small improvements in comfort and amenities

Super loud and exciting when the Yellow Jackets are on point, you won't be disappointed with your trip here.  Be ready to walk to and from since there really isn't any dedicated parking.

name: David Copeland
text:
My father is an Alumnus, having graduated in ‘61 with his Chemical Engineer degree.  Many years later, he would retire from International Paper.
He and my mom, a University of Georgia Alumnus saw many games here.  And afterwards, would consume tasty treats from The Varsity.
As kids, my older sister and I would share in their stories.  My visit to get drone shots was bittersweet because they’ve both died and this visit brought lots of memories.

name: Lobo L.
text:
Bobby Dodd Stadium is an historic, outdoor venue that was a great place to enjoy a beautiful college graduation. The location could benefit from some upgrades to guest seating areas where there was lots of cracked, crumbling concrete and hard, aluminum benches. $4 (12 oz.) bottled water at the concession stands, along with other exorbitantly priced snacks, should help cover the costs.

On the day of our event, stadium parking lots weren't available for guests, only graduating students. Nearby parking lot access was $10-$20 with a modest stroll to the stadium.

name: Diane Sachs
text:
SEATING and TICKETS
The views are great regardless of where you sit. Due metal seats and hot Georgia  sun, some sections are hellishly hot all game long, while others are shaded once the sun goes behind the west stands. Stinger Seats (seats with padding and backrests) are recommended or bring a cushion. Check the rules beforehand so you bring the right kind. The season ticket process is orderly and works with us to try and get the seats we want.

MUSIC and SOUND
The Tech spirit is high with the great Georgia Tech marching band in the north end zone. This year the band director has coordinated with the recorded music folks so each now plays without overlapping the other.

Traditional spirit songs and cheers as well as motivational music for the players mix well. Tech has a lot of traditions and band music is a key part of that tradition.  Acoustics are good, however the volume of the loud speakers can be painful at times. To their credit, when we asked the season ticket rep to pass this on, the sound was much more bearable at the following game.

STAFF
All stadium staff are friendly, polite and helpful. The contract group Wess Security is professional and quick to handle any issue that arises without making fans feel like we are under intrusive scrutiny all the time. The GT police are and have been (as long as I can remember from my student days) professional, friendly, helpful and good at what they do.

CONCESSIONS and FACILITIES
Food stands are evenly spread out with a fair variety of food and drinks in all sections.  Hurray! This year Non club seat members can purchase beer and drink it at our seats. Thank you stadium food and drink controllers.
Order and pay ahead pick up points are new and reduce lines and waiting. Season ticket holders get a discount.

The bathrooms are clean and as a lady, I can say the wait is not too bad if you time it properly.

The stadium is in good repair, clean and otherwise pleasant to be in.

AREA OF CONCERN
My only concern is access for impaired but not wheel chair bound guests. Elevators are not available for use.  I have difficulty walking up and down stairs and even ramps are hard. Granted I have not asked about assistance but I am supposing if they were available for us, it would be posted. That is the reason why I did not give it 5 stars
title: Bobby Dodd Stadium at Hyundai Field

name: Don Ross
text:
The stadium is old and has seen better days.  The upper deck is particularly cramped.  Once we couldn't all fit in our row of 12 bench seats and one guy had to sit on the steps.  When the crowd stands up, which is frequent, you have to turn sideways or someone ends up out on the steps.  The concessions are typical of college football stadiums.
It is located amongst the campus buildings and there is tailgating in the outdoor parking lots, i.e. not the garages.  We typically meet our friends who have an RV and it is a lot of fun.  You can also rent catered tailgating areas right by the stadium for events for 40-60 people.

text: Had a great time at Bobby Dodd Stadium! Our Georgia Tech Yellow Jackets pulled out a victory against virginia in overtime. Even though I was sitting almost on the exact opposite side of the field as the band, we could still hear every song played while at the same time still able to hear everything going  on with the game. As you come through the gates at Bobby Dodd, the staff as well as the volunteer students make you feel right at home. You can feel the Georgia Tech comradery in the air. The food offered at Grant field was great too with a lot of variety including Sonny's, Chic-Fil-A, Papa John's, and many others. My only complaint is that the seating is so tight around the field level its almost impossible to get from your seat to the concessions during the game.

name: Héctor López
text: Bobby Dodd Stadium at Historic Grant Field is the football stadium located at the corner of North Avenue at Techwood Drive on the campus of the Georgia Institute of Technology in Atlanta. It has been home to the Georgia Tech Yellow Jacketsfootball team, often referred to as the "Ramblin' Wreck", in rudimentary form since 1905 and as a complete stadium since 1913. The team participates in the NCAA Division I Football Bowl Subdivision as a member of the Atlantic Coast Conference. It is the oldest stadium in the FBS and has been the site of more home wins than any other FBS stadium.

text: Stadium for the Georgia Tech Yellow Jackets football, it houses up to 50,000 in open air bench seating. Rather out of place on the brick and steel aesthetic of the rest of the university, and greatly lacking sun protection, be sure to bring a change of clothes. Very standard for a large historic university, though perhaps disappointing for one specializing in engineering.

text: I realize this is the site for every other season of "Clean, Old-Fashioned Hate," but all that can really be said about this venue is "old-fashioned," nothing "clean" about it (especially the local area), and yes, I do "hate" it.  It's bad enough it only seats 55,000, but I guess when it's Tech, there really isn't much demand for a capacity crowd above that.  Still... it was fun watching my Dawgs record win #8 in a row here in 2015.
